info.title: Savonia.Assignment.Tool
info.description: Savonia.Assignment.Tool is an installable CLI tool to help with assignment handling.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show verbose output. [default: False]

commands.name: solution
commands.description: Work with your solution to an assignment

commands.name: pack
commands.description: Pack your solution to a zip file.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output zip file. This will overwrite possible existing file. By default the zip file is named the same as the source folder name.

options.name: --excludes
options.description: Folders and files to exclude. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --includes
options.description: Folders and files to include. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

commands.name: submissions
commands.description: Work with multiple solutions sent as answers

commands.name: unpack
commands.description: Unpack (unzip) all zip files in defined source folder. Creates a folder for each zip file where the contents are unpacked and overwrites possible existing files.

options.name: --characters
options.description: A list of characters to remove from unpacked folder names. Use single characters (i.e. , . -) or strings (i.e. ae ab) to remove occurrences of those in the created folder name. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

commands.name: test
commands.description: Work with tests on submissions

commands.name: copy
commands.description: Copy test harness or other assets to selected submissions.

options.name: --test-harness-target
options.description: Target directory in submissions folder to where the test harness is copied. Default is '' (i.e. the submission folder).

options.name: --excludes
options.description: Folders and files to exclude. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --includes
options.description: Folders and files to include. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --selected-submissions
options.description: Set index number of submission folders to copy the test harness to. Use submissions list command to see the numbers. Use single number (i.e. 3 4 8) to select individual folders. Use dash (-) to select a range of folders (i.e. 1-10 14-16) or use dash with one number to select from start or to end (i.e. -10 15-). Leave empty to select all folders. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

arguments.name: assetPath
arguments.description: Assets path from where to copy the files and folders to each submission folder.

arguments.name: submissionPath
arguments.description: Path to submission directory where to copy the test harness to each submission folder. [default: <path>]

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run tests for each selected submissions.

options.name: --test-data-prefix
options.description: Prefix to add to test data file names before reading the test data. Is used to run tests with different test data. [default: ]

options.name: --test-log-file
options.description: Test log file name used to write and read test results. The file is relative to TestResults folder in each test directory. [default: savoniatool.trx]

options.name: --test-summary-file
options.description: Test summary file name used to write test results JSON to each submission folder root. By default will overwrite possible existing file. [default: testrunsummary.json]

options.name: --overwrite-summary-file
options.description: Overwrite possible existing test summary file. Defaults to true. If set to false, will preserve existing file and append new results to it. This may cause duplicate test results and points in the file. [default: True]

options.name: --save-console-log
options.description: Save console output to a text file in the source folder. The file is named after the source folder with a timestamp (yyyy-MM-dd-HH-mm). Defaults to true. [default: True]

options.name: --max-parallel
options.description: Maximum number of submissions to test in parallel. Defaults to 0 which runs submissions sequentially. Recommended values: 3-5 for typical machines. Higher values may cause resource contention and instability in test runs, especially if the tests are resource intensive. [default: 0]

options.name: --selected-submissions
options.description: Set index number of submission folders to run tests on. Use 'submissions list' command to see the numbers. Use single number (i.e. 3 4 8) to select individual folders. Use dash (-) to select a range of folders (i.e. 1-10 14-16) or use dash with one number to select from start or to end (i.e. -10 15-). Leave empty to select all folders. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

arguments.name: testsJsonFile
arguments.description: JSON file containing test definitions. Use GitHub Classroom autograding json file definition.

commands.name: summary
commands.description: Collect test run summaries in submission folders and create summary file.

options.name: --test-summary-file
options.description: Test summary file name to read each submission's test run summary from. [default: testrunsummary.json]

options.name: --csv
options.description: Result summary file is CSV file by default. Set to false to create TSV file. [default: True]

options.name: --selected-submissions
options.description: Set index number of submission folders to include in summary. Use 'submissions list' command to see the numbers. Use single number (i.e. 3 4 8) to select individual folders. Use dash (-) to select a range of folders (i.e. 1-10 14-16) or use dash with one number to select from start or to end (i.e. -10 15-). Leave empty to select all folders. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --moodle-csv
options.description: A CSV file from Moodle LMS to combine the results with. When set, the results are combined to the defined file. The file is expected to contain submissions info from Moodle.

options.name: --moodle-csv-student-identifier-regex
options.description: A regex to get student identifier from submission folder name. [default: (?<=_)[0-9]+(?=_)]

options.name: --moodle-csv-student-identifier-prefix
options.description: Prefix to append to the student identifier to match a row in the Moodle CSV file. By default this is 'Opiskelija' for Finnish CSV file and 'Participant ' for English CSV file (notice the white space in the English version!). [default: Opiskelija]

options.name: --moodle-csv-append-id-to-feedback
options.description: Append student identifier to the feedback field in Moodle CSV file. [default: False]

options.name: --moodle-csv-feedback-id-prefix
options.description: Prefix text to add to student identifier in feedback field. [default: ]

options.name: --moodle-csv-feedback-id-suffix
options.description: Suffix text to add to student identifier in feedback field. [default: ]

arguments.name: resultSummaryCsvFile
arguments.description: Result summary file. By default the csv file is named the same as the source folder name.

commands.name: convert
commands.description: Convert test run summaries in submission folders to md files.

options.name: --title
options.description: Title for the md file. [default: Test run summary]

options.name: --test-summary-file
options.description: Test summary file name to read each submission's test run summary from. [default: testrunsummary.json]

options.name: --selected-submissions
options.description: Set index number of submission folders to include in summary. Use 'submissions list' command to see the numbers. Use single number (i.e. 3 4 8) to select individual folders. Use dash (-) to select a range of folders (i.e. 1-10 14-16) or use dash with one number to select from start or to end (i.e. -10 15-). Leave empty to select all folders. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --md-file-prefix
options.description: A prefix text to append to the created md file name.

options.name: --md-file-regex
options.description: A regex to get student identifier from submission folder name. [default: (?<=_)[0-9]+(?=_)]

options.name: --md-file-suffix
options.description: A suffix text to append to the created md file name.

options.name: --md-create-pdf
options.description: Create also a PDF file from created markdown content. [default: False]

options.name: --md-create-pdf-keep-html
options.description: When creating PDF files, keep the HTML file. [default: False]

arguments.name: destinationPath
arguments.description: Destination path where to save to md files. Default value is the folder where the test run summary file is read.

commands.name: pack
commands.description: Pack (zip) all submission folders to zip files in defined source folder. Creates a zip file for each folder and packs the content of the folder excluding the folder itself. Uses the folder name as the name of the zip file and overwrites possible existing file.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output zip file. When defined the included result is packed to a single zip file. Can be used to pack all submission zip files to a single zip file. This will overwrite possible existing file.

options.name: --includes
options.description: Folders and files to include. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --excludes
options.description: Folders and files to exclude. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

commands.name: list
commands.description: List submission folders in defined source folder. The list numbers can be used to select submissions to include in testing.

commands.name: open
commands.description: Open each submission in defined submissions folder one by one. Proceeds to the next submission when the editor is closed.

options.name: --unhandled-only
options.description: Open only submission within selected submissions that are not marked as handled. Set to 'false' to open all selected submissions. [default: True]

options.name: --mark-handled
options.description: Mark submissions as handled when moving to next submissions. [default: True]

options.name: --selected-submissions
options.description: Set index number of submission folders to run tests on. Use submissions list command to see the numbers. Use single number (i.e. 3 4 8) to select individual folders. Use dash (-) to select a range of folders (i.e. 1-10 14-16) or use dash with one number to select from start or to end (i.e. -10 15-). Leave empty to select all folders. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --editor-params
options.description: Parameters for the editor executable. [default: -n -w]

options.name: --editor
options.description: Editor executable name. The executable should be in the PATH environment variable. [default: code]

commands.name: hash
commands.description: Create hashes from files and operate on them.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create comparable hashes of code files in defined folder.

options.name: --includes
options.description: Folders and files to include. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --excludes
options.description: Folders and files to exclude. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --regex-filters
options.description: Regex filters to find code comments. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --starting-block-comment
options.description: Block comment start string [default: /*]

options.name: --starting-line-comment
options.description: Line comment start string [default: //]

options.name: --filter-source-code
options.description: Filter files before hashing. [default: All]

options.name: --folder-hash
options.description: Combine selected files in folder to one hash. [default: False]

arguments.name: hashesOutput
arguments.description: Output csv file. This will overwrite possible existing file. By default the csv file is named the same as the source folder name with "-hashes.csv" appended to the end.

commands.name: compare
commands.description: Compare hashes from source file.

options.name: --hash-index
options.description: Column index (zero-based) for the hash column. Null value assumes that hash is in the last column.

arguments.name: compareOutput
arguments.description: Output csv file. This will overwrite possible existing file. By default the output file is named the same as source file with "-compare-result.csv" appended to the end.

commands.name: open
commands.description: Open match groups with defined editor.

options.name: --hash-index
options.description: Column index (zero-based) for the hash column. Null value assumes that hash is in the last column.

options.name: --file-index
options.description: Column index (zero-based) for the file column. Null value assumes that file is in the first column.

options.name: --editor
options.description: Editor executable name. The executable should be in the PATH environment variable. [default: code]

options.name: --editor-params
options.description: Parameters for the editor executable. [default: -n]

commands.name: csv
commands.description: Work with CSV (Comma-separated values) files

commands.name: parse
commands.description: Parse a CSV file with selected fields and possible Regex filters and output to another CSV file.

options.name: --input-delimiter
options.description: Delimiter for the input CSV file. [default: ,]

options.name: --output-delimiter
options.description: Delimiter for the output CSV file. [default: ,]

options.name: --input-has-header
options.description: Set to true when the input CSV file has header row. [default: True]

options.name: --output
options.description: Output csv file. This will overwrite possible existing file. By default the output file is named the same as source file with "-result.csv" appended to the end.

options.name: --fields
options.description: Fields to select from the input CSV file to be written to output CSV file. Use one-based (i.e. first field is one (1)) field number or field header to select. Leave to empty to select all fields. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --field-filters
options.description: Field regex filter. Set in pairs where first value is field number or name and the second is the regex (e.g. --field-filters 3 \d+ to select first numbers from field 3). Use predefined regex filter values: URL to filter urls or regex name(s) defined in --regexes option. Leave to empty to select field data as is. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --regexes
options.description: A JSON file containing key-value pair (a dictionary) of regexes to filter/parse CSV column values. Key is the regex "name" and value is the regex "pattern".

commands.name: read
commands.description: Read a CSV file and write content to terminal.

options.name: --input-has-header
options.description: Set to true when the input CSV file has header row. [default: True]

options.name: --input-delimiter
options.description: Delimiter for the input CSV file. [default: ,]

commands.name: files
commands.description: Work with files

commands.name: copy
commands.description: Copy files and folders. Can be used to copy selected files and folder from a solution to a new location. Preserves folder structure.

options.name: --excludes
options.description: Folders and files to exclude. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --includes
options.description: Folders and files to include. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

arguments.name: destinationPath
arguments.description: Destination path where to copy the files and folders. If the path does not exist it will be created.

commands.name: pack
commands.description: Pack selected files and folders to a zip package. Preserves folder structure.

options.name: --excludes
options.description: Folders and files to exclude. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --includes
options.description: Folders and files to include. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

arguments.name: destinationZipFile
arguments.description: Destination zip file. By default the zip file is named the same as the source folder name.

commands.name: unpack
commands.description: Unpack files and folders from a zip package. Preserves folder structure.

arguments.name: destinationPath
arguments.description: Destination path to unpack the zip file. Default value is the zip file name without extension in current folder.

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Delete files. Can be used to delete selected files.

options.name: --excludes
options.description: Folders and files to exclude. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --includes
options.description: Folders and files to include. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --list
options.description: List only. Defaults to true to lists files that would be deleted. Does not delete files! To delete files, use --list false (or -l false). [default: True]

commands.name: folders
commands.description: Work with folders

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Delete folders. Can be used to delete selected folders. Note that this command matches folders by their files. If a folder contains files that match the include/exclude patterns, the folder will be deleted. If a folder does not contain any files that match the include/exclude patterns, the folder will not be deleted.

options.name: --excludes
options.description: Folders and files to exclude. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --includes
options.description: Folders and files to include. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --list
options.description: List only. Defaults to true to list folders that would be deleted. Does not delete folders! To delete folders, use --list false (or -l false). [default: True]

commands.name: test
commands.description: Work with (unit)testing

commands.name: trxtomd
commands.description: Converts a test result file (.trx) to a Markdown file (.md)

arguments.name: destinationMdFile
arguments.description: Destination file md file. Default value is the trx file name with .md extension in current folder.

commands.name: learn
commands.description: Work with MS Learn and other content

commands.name: read
commands.description: Read user's public MS Learn achievements.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output json file. This will overwrite possible existing file.

options.name: --key-value-pair
options.description: Write output as key-value-pair where the key is achievement id and value is achievement title. Default is false. [default: False]

arguments.name: username
arguments.description: The username of the user to read achievements for.

commands.name: check
commands.description: Check that required achievements are completed for users.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output csv file. This will overwrite possible existing file. By default the output file is named the same as source file with "-result.csv" appended to the end.

options.name: --input-has-header
options.description: Set to true when the input CSV file has header row. [default: True]

options.name: --input-delimiter
options.description: Delimiter for the input CSV file. [default: ,]

options.name: --username-field
options.description: Field where the username is read. Use one-based (i.e. first field is one (1)) field number or field header to select. Defaults to field number 9 which is default field for Moodle LMS export file for assingment submissions text. [default: 9]

options.name: --grade-field
options.description: Field where the grade is written. Use one-based (i.e. first field is one (1)) field number or field header to select. Defaults to field number 5 which is default field for Moodle LMS export file for assingment grade. [default: 5]

options.name: --grade
options.description: Grade value to write when all requirements are met. Defaults to number 1. [default: 1]

options.name: --comments-field
options.description: Field where the comments is written. Use one-based (i.e. first field is one (1)) field number or field header to select. Defaults to field number 11 which is default field for Moodle LMS export file for assingment feedback comments. [default: 11]

options.name: --field-filters
options.description: Field regex filter. Set in pairs where first value is field number or name and the second is the regex (e.g. --field-filters 3 \d+ to select first numbers from field 3). Use predefined regex filter values: URL to filter urls or regex name(s) defined in --regexes option. Leave to empty to select field data as is. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --regexes
options.description: A JSON file containing key-value pair (a dictionary) of regexes to filter/parse CSV column values. Key is the regex "name" and value is the regex "pattern".

arguments.name: requirementsJsonFile
arguments.description: JSON file containing required achievements as JSON key-value-pairs where the key is achievements type id and value is achievement title.

commands.name: content
commands.description: Check that required content are found from a web resource.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output csv file. This will overwrite possible existing file. By default the output file is named the same as source file with "-result.csv" appended to the end.

options.name: --resources-output-folder
options.description: Folder for loaded resource files. Can be absolute or relative path. By default the resources are not saved.

options.name: --input-has-header
options.description: Set to true when the input CSV file has header row. [default: True]

options.name: --input-delimiter
options.description: Delimiter for the input CSV file. [default: ,]

options.name: --name-field
options.description: Field where the name is read. Use one-based (i.e. first field is one (1)) field number or field header to select. Defaults to field number 2 which is default field for Moodle LMS export file for name. [default: 2]

options.name: --resource-field
options.description: Field where the resource URL is read. Use one-based (i.e. first field is one (1)) field number or field header to select. Defaults to field number 9 which is default field for Moodle LMS export file for assignment submissions text. [default: 9]

options.name: --grade-field
options.description: Field where the grade is written. Use one-based (i.e. first field is one (1)) field number or field header to select. Defaults to field number 5 which is default field for Moodle LMS export file for assignment grade. [default: 5]

options.name: --grade
options.description: Grade value to write when all requirements are met. Defaults to number 1. [default: 1]

options.name: --comments-field
options.description: Field where the comments is written. Use one-based (i.e. first field is one (1)) field number or field header to select. Defaults to field number 11 which is default field for Moodle LMS export file for assignment feedback comments. [default: 11]

options.name: --field-filters
options.description: Field regex filter. Set in pairs where first value is field number or name and the second is the regex (e.g. --field-filters 3 \d+ to select first numbers from field 3). Use predefined regex filter values: URL to filter urls or regex name(s) defined in --regexes option. Leave to empty to select field data as is. [default: System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]]

options.name: --regexes
options.description: A JSON file containing key-value pair (a dictionary) of regexes to filter/parse CSV column values. Key is the regex "name" and value is the regex "pattern".

arguments.name: requirementsJsonFile
arguments.description: JSON file containing the rules for the resource content.
